meta.name: Shawarma Spice Blend
meta.subtitle: Warm, aromatic Levantine-style spice mix for chicken, beef, lamb, veggies, and sauces

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Salt-free by default so it can be used flexibly (dry rub, marinades, sauces).
Scaled for a jar batch; includes optional heat and citrusy sumac.

summary: A classic shawarma-style spice blend: cumin-coriander backbone with paprika, turmeric, warm baking spices, and optional sumac/heat. Use as a dry rub or stir into oil + lemon for a quick marinade.

yield.notes: Makes about 1 1/2 cups (24 tbsp). Adjust batch size as needed.

optional.label: Spice grinder
optional.notes: Optional: grind whole spices fresh for maximum aroma.

optional.label: Fine-mesh sieve
optional.notes: Optional: sift if you want an ultra-smooth blend.

notes: Most spices are allergen-light, but always check for cross-contact warnings (especially paprika/chili powders).

ingredients.label: Paprika (sweet; smoked optional)
ingredients.notes: Use half smoked paprika if you like a deeper, BBQ-like note.

ingredients.label: Garlic powder
ingredients.notes: If you prefer, swap for granulated garlic.

ingredients.label: Onion powder
ingredients.notes: Optional, but gives a more 'shop' style rub.

ingredients.label: Ground cardamom
ingredients.notes: Use 1 tsp if you want it more subtle.

ingredients.label: Ground sumac
ingredients.notes: Adds bright tang; optional but highly recommended for a shawarma finish.

ingredients.label: Aleppo pepper (or mild chili flakes)
ingredients.notes: Optional heat. For hotter blend, use 1 tbsp cayenne instead.

ingredients.label: Cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: Only if you want the blend to have a built-in kick.

steps.title: Mix the spice blend
instructions: Add all spices to a bowl.
Whisk thoroughly until the color and texture are uniform.
Optional: sift for a smoother blend.

steps.title: Jar and label
instructions: Transfer the blend to a clean, dry jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store in a cool, dark place.

steps.title: How to use (quick formulas)
instructions: Dry rub (per 1 lb meat): 1 to 1 1/2 tbsp blend + 1 tsp kosher salt + 1 tbsp oil (optional). Rest 15–30 minutes, then cook hot.
Quick marinade (per 2 lb meat): 3 tbsp blend + 2 tsp kosher salt + 1/4 cup oil + 3 tbsp lemon juice + 3–4 minced garlic cloves. Marinate 45 min–6 hours.
Veg roast: Toss vegetables with 1 tbsp blend + 1/2 tsp salt + 1 tbsp oil per sheet pan; roast at 425–450°F.
Sauce booster: Stir 1–2 tsp blend into tahini sauce, yogurt sauce, or toum for instant shawarma flavor.
checks: Salt is added separately in usage formulas (blend is salt-free)

pantry.notes: Best flavor within 3 months. Keep dry and tightly sealed.

freezer.notes: Freezing extends aroma retention. Bring to room temp before opening to avoid condensation.

batchNotes: Make a double batch if your household does shawarma weekly.
Keep a small shaker jar in the everyday spice drawer and the bulk jar in pantry storage.

notes: If your ingredient IDs differ, map using ingredient aliases (e.g., paprika_smoked vs paprika).
If omitting sumac, add brightness later with lemon juice + a finishing sprinkle of sumac at serving.
Salt-free by design: salt varies by meat cut, cooking method, and dietary needs.
If you can, grind whole cumin/coriander fresh: 2 parts cumin seed : 2 parts coriander seed, toasted lightly, then ground and mixed with remaining spices.
If your paprika is old or dull, your blend will taste flat—fresh paprika matters here.
Include onion powder (2 tbsp) and garlic powder (increase to 4 tbsp).
Add 1 tbsp ground ginger for extra warmth.
Serve heat separately (hot sauce or chili flakes at the table).
Increase allspice to 2 tbsp and add 1 tbsp dried oregano.
Add 1 tsp ground rosemary (optional) for a piney lamb-friendly note.
